data.title: Tether Invests in Utexo to Enhance Bitcoin-Based USDT Payments
data.description: Tether has co-led a $7.5 million funding round in Utexo, a startup focused on developing infrastructure for USDT payments on the Bitcoin network. According to NS3.AI, Tether emphasizes that Bitcoin is integral to its long-term vision for USDT. Utexo aims to fill the gap in infrastructure needed for Bitcoin-based stablecoin payments, leveraging the security of the Bitcoin network. Settlement fees for these transactions will be paid in USDT.

data.title: Blue Owl Capital's OBDC II Fund Halts Redemptions Amid Liquidity Concerns
data.description: Blue Owl Capital's OBDC II private credit fund has permanently stopped redemptions and is transitioning to return-of-capital distributions. According to NS3.AI, this move is funded by loan repayments and approximately $1.4 billion in asset sales, underscoring liquidity stress in a market valued near $3 trillion. In contrast, Blackstone's BCRED fund reportedly addressed around $3.7 billion in first-quarter withdrawal requests by increasing redemption caps and injecting over $400 million of internal capital, avoiding gating. The article suggests that if liquidity pressure in private credit markets expands, large investors might initially sell highly liquid assets like Bitcoin, potentially causing a temporary decline. This could later reverse if the stress leads to more accommodative monetary policies.

data.title: Bitcoin Miner Cango Reduces Mining Capacity Amid Profitability Concerns
data.description: Bitcoin miner Cango has reduced its mining capacity by approximately 30%, lowering its average operating hashrate to 34.55 EH/s from the previously deployed 50 EH/s. According to NS3.AI, the company attributed this decision to declining mining profitability and stated that the reduction is part of its strategy to optimize equipment efficiency.

data.title: Petrobras CEO: Price Fluctuations Will Not Be Passed On
data.description: Petrobras, Brazil's state-controlled oil company, has announced that it will not pass on price fluctuations to consumers. According to Jin10, the company's CEO emphasized that Petrobras is committed to maintaining stable prices despite market volatility. This decision comes amid global economic uncertainties and fluctuating oil prices. The CEO's statement aims to reassure consumers and stakeholders about the company's pricing strategy in the current economic climate.

data.title: IG4 Capital May Withdraw from Braskem Acquisition Talks
data.description: Asset manager IG4 Capital has indicated it might end discussions to acquire a controlling interest in Brazilian petrochemical company Braskem. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the potential withdrawal from negotiations. The move comes amid ongoing talks and strategic evaluations by IG4 Capital regarding the acquisition. Braskem, a major player in the petrochemical industry, has been the subject of acquisition interest, but the latest developments suggest a shift in IG4 Capital's approach. The decision to potentially abandon the talks reflects the complexities and challenges involved in such high-stakes negotiations.

data.title: Bitcoin's Decline Amid U.S. Job Losses and Rising Unemployment
data.description: Bitcoin experienced a significant drop of over 5%, settling around $68,282, following the U.S. economy's loss of 92,000 jobs in February and an increase in the unemployment rate to 4.4%. According to NS3.AI, crypto derivatives liquidations reached approximately $370 million within 24 hours, with nearly half attributed to Bitcoin longs. Additionally, Bitcoin exchange-traded funds witnessed $228 million in outflows, reflecting cautious sentiment among institutional investors. Traders are now turning their attention to next week's economic calendar, which includes major inflation and labor data that could further impact risk assets like Bitcoin.

data.title: Brother Maji deposited another 210,000 USDC into Hyperliquid to increase his ETH long position.
data.description: Hyperbot data shows that Machi Dage deposited another 210,000 USDC into Hyperliquid to increase his 25x leveraged ETH long position. Prior to this, some of Machi Dage's ETH long positions were liquidated as the market declined.

data.title: EQB CEO Seeks Swift Approval for Major Acquisition to Compete in Canadian Banking Market
data.description: EQB's CEO has expressed the need for rapid approval of a significant acquisition to enhance competition in Canada's banking sector. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the CEO's ambition to expand EQB's presence and influence in the market. The acquisition is seen as a strategic move to bolster EQB's capabilities and offer more competitive options to consumers. The CEO emphasized the importance of timely regulatory approval to ensure the firm's growth and ability to challenge existing market players effectively. The acquisition is expected to bring new dynamics to the Canadian banking landscape, potentially benefiting customers with more choices and improved services.

data.title: TRON's Decentralization Questioned Amid Ownership Concerns
data.description: TRON's decentralization status is under scrutiny as reports suggest that Justin Sun, the founder, holds more TRX than all other holders combined. According to NS3.AI, this concentration of ownership raises questions about the network's governance. Despite these concerns, TRON's official materials and several major exchanges continue to describe TRON or TRX as decentralized or governed by a DAO. The article claims that no DAO actually governs the network, challenging the perception of TRON's decentralized nature.

data.title: Pakistan Establishes Virtual Asset Regulatory Authority with New Cryptocurrency Law
data.description: Pakistan's parliament has enacted a new cryptocurrency law, establishing the federal Virtual Asset Regulatory Authority (PVARA). According to NS3.AI, this regulatory body will oversee and license service providers, including exchanges and token issuers. The legislation imposes fines of up to $179,000 for operating unlicensed crypto businesses. Additionally, conducting such activities without a license is now a criminal offense, carrying potential prison sentences of up to five years.

data.title: U.S. Stock Market Experiences Sharp Decline Amid Employment Data and Rising Oil Prices
data.description:
U.S. stock markets saw a significant drop in early trading as last month's employment data showed a notable downturn. According to RTHK, non-farm payrolls unexpectedly decreased by 92,000, and the unemployment rate rose to 4.4%. Additionally, oil prices surged again, raising concerns about inflation and its potential impact on the economy.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell nearly 915 points after the market opened, testing the 47,000-point threshold, marking a three-month low. It was recently reported at 47,117 points, down 836 points, or approximately 1.8%. The S&P 500 Index stood at 6,725 points, down 105 points, with a decline of over 1.5%. Meanwhile, the Nasdaq Composite Index was at 22,425 points, down 323 points, with a drop of over 1.4%.

Oil prices experienced a significant surge, with London Brent crude surpassing $90 per barrel for the first time in nearly two years, recently reported at $91.51, up over 7%. New York crude futures earlier exceeded $89, reaching $89.3, up over 10%, marking a two-and-a-half-year high. British and American crude oil have increased by approximately 50% and nearly 56% respectively this year.

data.title: Iran Warns Iraqi Kurdistan Over Border Security Measures
data.description: Iran's military has issued a stern warning to the government of Iraq's Kurdistan region, stating that it will take decisive action against any deployment of hostile forces along the Iranian border. According to Jin10, the warning underscores Iran's commitment to maintaining security and stability in the region. The announcement comes amid ongoing tensions and geopolitical challenges in the Middle East, highlighting the sensitive nature of border security and regional relations. Iran's stance reflects its broader strategic interests and concerns over potential threats near its borders.

data.title: Iranian Revolutionary Guard Attacks US Bases in UAE and Kuwait
data.description: Iranian state media reports that the Iranian Revolutionary Guard has launched attacks on the US military stationed at the Al Dhafra Air Base in the United Arab Emirates and the Ali Al Salem Air Base in Kuwait. According to Jin10, these actions mark a significant escalation in regional tensions, potentially impacting geopolitical stability in the Middle East. The attacks come amid ongoing disputes and heightened military activities in the area, raising concerns about further conflict and its implications for international relations.

data.title: A trader opened a short position in WTI crude oil with 3x leverage, at a liquidation price of $114.88.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, WTI crude oil futures prices surged, but trader "0x4ff9" bet against the trend by opening a 3x leveraged short position in WTI crude oil with a liquidation price of $114.88. The current position holds 30,000 shares of WTI crude oil futures.

data.title: U.S. December Business Inventories Show Slight Increase
data.description: The U.S. December business inventories rose by 0.1%, aligning with expectations, according to Jin10. The previous value was revised from 0.10% to 0%, indicating a minor adjustment in the data. This slight increase in inventories suggests a stable economic environment, with businesses maintaining consistent stock levels. The revision of the prior month's data reflects ongoing adjustments in economic reporting, highlighting the importance of accurate inventory tracking for economic analysis.

data.title: Russian Urals Oil Delivery Price Surpasses Benchmark at Indian Ports
data.description: Russian Urals crude oil has seen its delivery price at Indian ports exceed the benchmark for the first time, according to Jin10. This development marks a significant shift in the pricing dynamics of Russian oil exports to India, reflecting changes in market conditions and demand. The price increase could have implications for global oil markets, as India is a major importer of Russian crude. Traders are closely monitoring the situation to assess its potential impact on future trade and pricing strategies.

data.title: Kevin Hassett Acknowledges Utility of Prediction Markets
data.description: White House National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett has expressed that prediction markets hold value in certain contexts. According to NS3.AI, Hassett's remarks highlight the potential applications of prediction markets in economic forecasting and decision-making processes. While he did not elaborate on specific uses, his acknowledgment suggests a recognition of their role in providing insights into market trends and public sentiment. Prediction markets, which allow participants to bet on the outcomes of future events, have been gaining attention for their ability to aggregate diverse opinions and information. Hassett's comments may indicate a growing interest in exploring these markets as tools for economic analysis and policy development.

data.title: BlackRock Limits Withdrawals Amid Rising Redemption Requests
data.description: BlackRock has restricted withdrawals from one of its largest private credit funds following a surge in client redemption requests. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting growing concerns among retail investors about the $1.8 trillion private credit sector. This move reflects increasing anxiety within the industry as investors seek to pull back their investments. The decision underscores the challenges faced by private credit funds in managing liquidity amid heightened demand for redemptions.

data.title: Trump's Iran Stance Impacts Markets, Bitcoin and Stocks Decline
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump declared that there will be "no deal with Iran except UNCONDITIONAL SURRENDER," which has influenced market movements. According to NS3.AI, this statement pushed WTI crude oil prices close to $90 per barrel, while causing declines in both bitcoin and stock markets. Nasdaq futures experienced a 1.8% drop, and bitcoin fell by 5% to approximately $68,800 as risk markets reacted negatively.

In addition to these developments, separate data revealed a decrease in U.S. payrolls by 92,000 jobs in February, with the unemployment rate rising to 4.4%. Despite the weak labor market data, interest rate markets are currently pricing in only a 4% probability of a Federal Reserve rate cut in March and a 17% chance of a cut in April.

data.title: U.S. Secretary of State Rubio Accuses Rwandan Officials of Fueling Instability in Eastern Congo
data.description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio has accused Rwandan officials of exacerbating the unrest in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC). According to Jin10, Rubio's statement highlights the ongoing tensions in the region, where various armed groups have been active. The eastern DRC has long been plagued by conflict, with neighboring countries often being implicated in the turmoil. Rubio's remarks underscore the international concern over the situation and the role of regional actors in either mitigating or aggravating the conflict. The U.S. continues to monitor the developments closely, emphasizing the need for diplomatic efforts to restore stability in the area.
